title: Elaeosticta aitchisonii
intro: Elaeosticta aitchisonii, commonly known as Aitchison's Elaeosticta, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Apiaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Elaeosticta aitchisonii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Elaeosticta aitchisonii is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly consumed to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and other respiratory conditions.

It is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia and Afghanistan. The plant is classified in the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Apiales, family Apiaceae, genus Elaeosticta, and species E. aitchisonii.
